title: Tangy Gooey Chinese Pork Bao
description: Sour, savory pork filling wrapped in warm potato buns, finished with a glossy tangy glaze—perfect for a comforting date‑night dinner.
instructions: In a bowl combine soy sauce, dry rice wine, brown sugar, rice vinegar, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, and half the scallions; whisk until sugar dissolves. Add pork cubes, toss to coat, and marinate for 15 min.
Heat a large skillet or grill pan over medium‑high heat. Add pork with its liquid, cooking 5–6 min until browned and caramelized, stirring frequently.
Stir in cornstarch mixed with 1 tbsp water to create a glossy sauce; simmer until thickened, about 2 min. Remove from heat and stir in remaining scallions.
Warm the potato buns on the grill or in a toaster oven for 1–2 min until lightly toasted.
Divide the pork filling among the buns, smother with extra sauce, and serve hot.
